title: Bridge Essay: A dual-brain therapeutic approach using noninvasive brain stimulation based on two-person neuroscience: A perspective review.
concept: Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.

**A dual-brain therapeutic approach using noninvasive brain stimulation based on two-person neuroscience: A perspective review.** - Math Biosci Eng (2024). DOI: 10.3934/mbe.2024226. PMID: 38872529. Our actions and decisions in everyday life are heavily influenced by social interactions, which are dynamic feedback loops involving actions, reactions, and internal cognitive processes between individual agents. Social interactions induce interpersonal synchrony, which occurs at different biobehavioral levels and comprises behavioral, physiological, and neurological activities.
